title: Notoseris glandulosa
taxonomy: Notoseris glandulosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of plants native to New Zealand. The specific classification of Notoseris glandulosa is as a member of the tribe Senecioneae. This classification suggests that Notoseris glandulosa is closely related to other plants within the same family and tribe. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification and relationships within the Notoseris genus.

intro: Notoseris glandulosa, also known as glandular Notoseris, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Notoseris glandulosa include triterpenoid saponins, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditional herbal preparations made with Notoseris glandulosa include infusions, decoctions, and capsules, often used in Chinese medicine to support immune function and relieve pain.
